articles.title: NMMC issues heatwave advisory - Newsband

articles.full_text:
NMMC issues heatwave advisory
Navi Mumbai: In view of rising temperatures and the possibility of severe heatwave conditions, the Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation (NMMC) has issued detailed guidelines for its officers and employees to prevent heat-related illnesses during the summer season.
The advisory comes amid growing concern over extreme heat across the country. Authorities noted that “Heat Wave” has now been formally recognised as a notified disaster in the disaster management plans of several Indian states. Maharashtra is also considered among the top ten most heat-vulnerable states and Union Territories in the country.
According to the civic body, updated guidelines issued during 2025-26 by national agencies, including the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), India Meteorological Department (IMD), Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW), and the Ministry of Labour and Employment, have been taken into consideration while preparing the advisory.
The NMMC has instructed field staff and officials working outdoors to avoid going out in direct sunlight between 12 noon and 4 pm as far as possible. Employees have been advised to carry out fieldwork before 11 am or after 4 pm. However, in case of emergency duties, officials have been asked to take all necessary precautions during heat alert periods.
The civic body also directed department heads to ensure the safety of contractual and temporary workers working at the field level during periods of extreme heat. Officials and employees have been advised to stay hydrated by drinking sufficient water and consuming fluids such as ORS, lemon water, and buttermilk. The guidelines also recommend wearing “light-coloured, loose and cotton clothes” and using caps, scarves, or umbrellas while stepping outdoors.
Department heads have been further instructed to ensure the availability of cool drinking water and first-aid facilities at workplaces.
The advisory warned employees to seek immediate medical attention if symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, headache, excessive sweating, or weakness are experienced. It also directed department heads to take prompt action if any employee’s health deteriorates during working hours.

├─────────────────────────┤
│ • Avoid sun: 12pm-4pm │
│ • Drink water & ORS │
│ • Wear cotton clothes │
│ • Use a cap/umbrella │
